hypothesis: In MADE configured with both MLIP and DFT oracles, a multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization agent that fits a calibrated surrogate to both fidelities and selects DFT queries by maximizing the expected number of new DFT-level convex-hull vertices will discover ≥50% more novel DFT-stable compounds than a MatterGen+diversity baseline under a fixed DFT budget of 50 evaluations on held-out ternary systems.
